reasoning: The gem_ruby_v00 asset renders as a loose cluster of ~20 small pink/rose pixel fragments scattered across a ~120x120px area. Against the dark navy/charcoal background panel it is highly spottable within 0.5s purely due to the saturated pink hue, which is unique in the scene. Color contrast and distinguishment from other entities (gold UI badges, teal player orb, red/blue/orange action buttons) are strong. However, the asset lacks a coherent single silhouette — it reads more as a particle explosion or damage spray than a discrete collectible gem. Individual fragment pieces are 4-8px, creating visual noise rather than a clean icon. In a fast-paced context the player may not immediately parse it as a gem pickup. The pixel art style is consistent with the game aesthetic. No background hue clash. Placement on the mid-tone dark panel keeps it readable. Score capped at 6 (marginal) because silhouette clarity and collectible readability fall short of an 8+ pass threshold, even though locatability by color alone is adequate.
